{{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[sn:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[Category:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] }} [[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] [[sn:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] [[ja:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==
'''Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe) (Q11559098)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Akashi District, Hyōgo|en|Akashi District, Hyōgo|ja|明石郡|zh|明石郡|lt=Akashi district|WD=Q11512561}} || {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|lt=Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|en|Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|ja|海神社 (神戸市)|commons|Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)|simple|Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|WD=Q11559098}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] ({{ill|Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|WD=Q135009132|lt=Tsukinami}}{{efn|gets offerings for the yearly {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|WD=Q11501518}} and the monthly {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|simple|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|WD=Q11516161}}.}}) || {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} || 4 seats || — || {{coord|34.628724|135.054257|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183009 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]
== Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)]][[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[ja:海神社 (神戸市)]][[simple:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11559098]] Wikidata name: Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine to the Sea God Watatsumi}} {{Citation style|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Watatsumi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg | deity = {{ill|Q112369714|WD=Q112369714}}; {{ill|Q112369870|WD=Q112369870}}; {{ill|Q112369995|WD=Q112369995}} | website = https://kaijinjya.main.jp/ | prefecture= {{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.629083|N|135.054306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyōgo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
It is colloquially called {{Nihongo|Sea Shrine|海神社|Umi Jinja}} due to that being a more common reading of the characters.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It is also read as Kai Shrine or called Tarumi Shrine.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}
== History ==
According to the shrine's legend, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was nearly shipwrecked when returning to Japan from a [[Empress Jingū#Korean Invasion|military conquest in Korea]]. She survived thanks to praying to [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], and made the shrine to honor him.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> There are 22 Watatsumi shrines in the region that claim to have been founded by her in these circumstances.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} [[Ikasuri Shrine]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]] were both also made at the same time by the Empress.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> The son of Tomomi no Sukune who accompanied the Empress on her expedition became the first priest of the shrine.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |pages=335 |language=English}}</ref>
In 806 AD, the shrine was financially supported by taxes from ten households.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]], the highest Rank of significant shrines in the 927 AD [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Watatsumi+Shrine |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] it gained a lot of significance for the rulers of the [[:en:Akashi Domain|Akashi Domain]], who visited it every February.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
Originally its main [[Torii]] was located on the beach like [[Itsukushima Shrine]], but due to land reclamation after [[:en:WWII|WWII]] its torii is now a fair bit inland.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> ==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Kai-jinja Shrine in Tarumi, Kobe File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|alt=|Donation box File:Kobe-wtatumijinjya76.JPG|alt=|Honden File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii.jpg|alt=|Torii File:Museum of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Exterior view of Museum of Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Inari Shrine, Ebisu Shrine and Sarutahiko Shrine in Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gates of Inari, Ebisu, Sarutahiko Shrines in Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gate of Watatsumi Shrine File:View in Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|View within Watatsumi Shrine File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine parking lot.jpg|alt=|Watatsumi Shrine grounds File:View of Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden in Watatsumi Shrine File:Wadatsumi temple.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Watatusmi-Jinja.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine view of Haiden through Torii from street.jpg|alt=|Torii File:海神社鳥居 Gateway to Watatsumi-jinja shrine.jpg|alt=|Large Torii similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]] </gallery>

'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[Empress Jingu]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyogo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />

{{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kobe]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[sn:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[Category:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[Category:Pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] }} [[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] [[sn:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] [[ja:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==
'''Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe) (Q11559098)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Akashi District, Hyōgo|en|Akashi District, Hyōgo|ja|明石郡|zh|明石郡|lt=Akashi district|WD=Q11512561}} || {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|lt=Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|en|Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|ja|海神社 (神戸市)|commons|Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)|simple|Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|WD=Q11559098}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] ({{ill|Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|WD=Q135009132|lt=Tsukinami}}{{efn|gets offerings for the yearly {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|WD=Q11501518}} and the monthly {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|simple|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|WD=Q11516161}}.}}) || {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} || 4 seats || — || {{coord|34.628724|135.054257|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183009 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]
== Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)]][[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[ja:海神社 (神戸市)]][[simple:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11559098]] Wikidata name: Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine to the Sea God Watatsumi}} {{Citation style|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Watatsumi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg | deity = {{ill|Q112369714|WD=Q112369714}}; {{ill|Q112369870|WD=Q112369870}}; {{ill|Q112369995|WD=Q112369995}} | website = https://kaijinjya.main.jp/ | prefecture= {{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.629083|N|135.054306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyōgo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
It is colloquially called {{Nihongo|Sea Shrine|海神社|Umi Jinja}} due to that being a more common reading of the characters.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It is also read as Kai Shrine or called Tarumi Shrine.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}
== History ==
According to the shrine's legend, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was nearly shipwrecked when returning to Japan from a [[Empress Jingū#Korean Invasion|military conquest in Korea]]. She survived thanks to praying to [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], and made the shrine to honor him.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> There are 22 Watatsumi shrines in the region that claim to have been founded by her in these circumstances.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} [[Ikasuri Shrine]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]] were both also made at the same time by the Empress.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> The son of Tomomi no Sukune who accompanied the Empress on her expedition became the first priest of the shrine.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |pages=335 |language=English}}</ref>
In 806 AD, the shrine was financially supported by taxes from ten households.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]], the highest Rank of significant shrines in the 927 AD [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Watatsumi+Shrine |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] it gained a lot of significance for the rulers of the [[:en:Akashi Domain|Akashi Domain]], who visited it every February.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
Originally its main [[Torii]] was located on the beach like [[Itsukushima Shrine]], but due to land reclamation after [[:en:WWII|WWII]] its torii is now a fair bit inland.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> ==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Kai-jinja Shrine in Tarumi, Kobe File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|alt=|Donation box File:Kobe-wtatumijinjya76.JPG|alt=|Honden File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii.jpg|alt=|Torii File:Museum of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Exterior view of Museum of Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Inari Shrine, Ebisu Shrine and Sarutahiko Shrine in Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gates of Inari, Ebisu, Sarutahiko Shrines in Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gate of Watatsumi Shrine File:View in Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|View within Watatsumi Shrine File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine parking lot.jpg|alt=|Watatsumi Shrine grounds File:View of Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden in Watatsumi Shrine File:Wadatsumi temple.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Watatusmi-Jinja.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine view of Haiden through Torii from street.jpg|alt=|Torii File:海神社鳥居 Gateway to Watatsumi-jinja shrine.jpg|alt=|Large Torii similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]] </gallery>

'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[Empress Jingu]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyogo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />

== Accidentally Overwritten Content ==
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)]][[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[ja:海神社 (神戸市)]][[simple:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11559098]] Wikidata name: Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine to the Sea God Watatsumi}} {{Citation style|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Watatsumi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg | deity = {{ill|Q112369714|WD=Q112369714}}; {{ill|Q112369870|WD=Q112369870}}; {{ill|Q112369995|WD=Q112369995}} | website = https://kaijinjya.main.jp/ | prefecture= {{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.629083|N|135.054306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyōgo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
It is colloquially called {{Nihongo|Sea Shrine|海神社|Umi Jinja}} due to that being a more common reading of the characters.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It is also read as Kai Shrine or called Tarumi Shrine.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}
== History ==
According to the shrine's legend, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was nearly shipwrecked when returning to Japan from a [[Empress Jingū#Korean Invasion|military conquest in Korea]]. She survived thanks to praying to [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], and made the shrine to honor him.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> There are 22 Watatsumi shrines in the region that claim to have been founded by her in these circumstances.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} [[Ikasuri Shrine]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]] were both also made at the same time by the Empress.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> The son of Tomomi no Sukune who accompanied the Empress on her expedition became the first priest of the shrine.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |pages=335 |language=English}}</ref>
In 806 AD, the shrine was financially supported by taxes from ten households.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]], the highest Rank of significant shrines in the 927 AD [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Watatsumi+Shrine |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] it gained a lot of significance for the rulers of the [[:en:Akashi Domain|Akashi Domain]], who visited it every February.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
Originally its main [[Torii]] was located on the beach like [[Itsukushima Shrine]], but due to land reclamation after [[:en:WWII|WWII]] its torii is now a fair bit inland.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> ==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Kai-jinja Shrine in Tarumi, Kobe File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|alt=|Donation box File:Kobe-wtatumijinjya76.JPG|alt=|Honden File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii.jpg|alt=|Torii File:Museum of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Exterior view of Museum of Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Inari Shrine, Ebisu Shrine and Sarutahiko Shrine in Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gates of Inari, Ebisu, Sarutahiko Shrines in Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gate of Watatsumi Shrine File:View in Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|View within Watatsumi Shrine File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine parking lot.jpg|alt=|Watatsumi Shrine grounds File:View of Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden in Watatsumi Shrine File:Wadatsumi temple.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Watatusmi-Jinja.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine view of Haiden through Torii from street.jpg|alt=|Torii File:海神社鳥居 Gateway to Watatsumi-jinja shrine.jpg|alt=|Large Torii similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]] </gallery>

== Accidentally Overwritten Content ==
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)]][[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[ja:海神社 (神戸市)]][[simple:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11559098]] Wikidata name: Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine to the Sea God Watatsumi}} {{Citation style|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Watatsumi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg | deity = {{ill|Q112369714|WD=Q112369714}}; {{ill|Q112369870|WD=Q112369870}}; {{ill|Q112369995|WD=Q112369995}} | website = https://kaijinjya.main.jp/ | prefecture= {{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.629083|N|135.054306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyōgo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
It is colloquially called {{Nihongo|Sea Shrine|海神社|Umi Jinja}} due to that being a more common reading of the characters.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It is also read as Kai Shrine or called Tarumi Shrine.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}
== History ==
According to the shrine's legend, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was nearly shipwrecked when returning to Japan from a [[Empress Jingū#Korean Invasion|military conquest in Korea]]. She survived thanks to praying to [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], and made the shrine to honor him.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> There are 22 Watatsumi shrines in the region that claim to have been founded by her in these circumstances.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} [[Ikasuri Shrine]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]] were both also made at the same time by the Empress.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> The son of Tomomi no Sukune who accompanied the Empress on her expedition became the first priest of the shrine.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |pages=335 |language=English}}</ref>
In 806 AD, the shrine was financially supported by taxes from ten households.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]], the highest Rank of significant shrines in the 927 AD [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Watatsumi+Shrine |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] it gained a lot of significance for the rulers of the [[:en:Akashi Domain|Akashi Domain]], who visited it every February.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
Originally its main [[Torii]] was located on the beach like [[Itsukushima Shrine]], but due to land reclamation after [[:en:WWII|WWII]] its torii is now a fair bit inland.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> ==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Kai-jinja Shrine in Tarumi, Kobe File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|alt=|Donation box File:Kobe-wtatumijinjya76.JPG|alt=|Honden File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii.jpg|alt=|Torii File:Museum of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Exterior view of Museum of Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Inari Shrine, Ebisu Shrine and Sarutahiko Shrine in Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gates of Inari, Ebisu, Sarutahiko Shrines in Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gate of Watatsumi Shrine File:View in Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|View within Watatsumi Shrine File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine parking lot.jpg|alt=|Watatsumi Shrine grounds File:View of Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden in Watatsumi Shrine File:Wadatsumi temple.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Watatusmi-Jinja.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine view of Haiden through Torii from street.jpg|alt=|Torii File:海神社鳥居 Gateway to Watatsumi-jinja shrine.jpg|alt=|Large Torii similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]] </gallery>

== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)]][[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[ja:海神社 (神戸市)]][[simple:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11559098]] Wikidata name: Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine to the Sea God Watatsumi}} {{Citation style|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Watatsumi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg | deity = {{ill|Q112369714|WD=Q112369714}}; {{ill|Q112369870|WD=Q112369870}}; {{ill|Q112369995|WD=Q112369995}} | website = https://kaijinjya.main.jp/ | prefecture= {{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.629083|N|135.054306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyōgo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
It is colloquially called {{Nihongo|Sea Shrine|海神社|Umi Jinja}} due to that being a more common reading of the characters.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It is also read as Kai Shrine or called Tarumi Shrine.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}
== History ==
According to the shrine's legend, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was nearly shipwrecked when returning to Japan from a [[Empress Jingū#Korean Invasion|military conquest in Korea]]. She survived thanks to praying to [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], and made the shrine to honor him.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> There are 22 Watatsumi shrines in the region that claim to have been founded by her in these circumstances.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} [[Ikasuri Shrine]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]] were both also made at the same time by the Empress.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> The son of Tomomi no Sukune who accompanied the Empress on her expedition became the first priest of the shrine.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |pages=335 |language=English}}</ref>
In 806 AD, the shrine was financially supported by taxes from ten households.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]], the highest Rank of significant shrines in the 927 AD [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Watatsumi+Shrine |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] it gained a lot of significance for the rulers of the [[:en:Akashi Domain|Akashi Domain]], who visited it every February.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
Originally its main [[Torii]] was located on the beach like [[Itsukushima Shrine]], but due to land reclamation after [[:en:WWII|WWII]] its torii is now a fair bit inland.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> ==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Kai-jinja Shrine in Tarumi, Kobe File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|alt=|Donation box File:Kobe-wtatumijinjya76.JPG|alt=|Honden File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii.jpg|alt=|Torii File:Museum of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Exterior view of Museum of Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Inari Shrine, Ebisu Shrine and Sarutahiko Shrine in Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gates of Inari, Ebisu, Sarutahiko Shrines in Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gate of Watatsumi Shrine File:View in Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|View within Watatsumi Shrine File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine parking lot.jpg|alt=|Watatsumi Shrine grounds File:View of Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden in Watatsumi Shrine File:Wadatsumi temple.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Watatusmi-Jinja.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine view of Haiden through Torii from street.jpg|alt=|Torii File:海神社鳥居 Gateway to Watatsumi-jinja shrine.jpg|alt=|Large Torii similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]] </gallery>

'''Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe) (Q11559098)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)]][[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[ja:海神社 (神戸市)]][[simple:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11559098]] Wikidata name: Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine to the Sea God Watatsumi}} {{Citation style|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Watatsumi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg | deity = {{ill|Q112369714|WD=Q112369714}}; {{ill|Q112369870|WD=Q112369870}}; {{ill|Q112369995|WD=Q112369995}} | website = https://kaijinjya.main.jp/ | prefecture= {{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.629083|N|135.054306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyōgo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
It is colloquially called {{Nihongo|Sea Shrine|海神社|Umi Jinja}} due to that being a more common reading of the characters.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It is also read as Kai Shrine or called Tarumi Shrine.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}
== History ==
According to the shrine's legend, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was nearly shipwrecked when returning to Japan from a [[Empress Jingū#Korean Invasion|military conquest in Korea]]. She survived thanks to praying to [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], and made the shrine to honor him.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> There are 22 Watatsumi shrines in the region that claim to have been founded by her in these circumstances.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} [[Ikasuri Shrine]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]] were both also made at the same time by the Empress.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> The son of Tomomi no Sukune who accompanied the Empress on her expedition became the first priest of the shrine.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |pages=335 |language=English}}</ref>
In 806 AD, the shrine was financially supported by taxes from ten households.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]], the highest Rank of significant shrines in the 927 AD [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Watatsumi+Shrine |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] it gained a lot of significance for the rulers of the [[:en:Akashi Domain|Akashi Domain]], who visited it every February.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
Originally its main [[Torii]] was located on the beach like [[Itsukushima Shrine]], but due to land reclamation after [[:en:WWII|WWII]] its torii is now a fair bit inland.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> ==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Kai-jinja Shrine in Tarumi, Kobe File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|alt=|Donation box File:Kobe-wtatumijinjya76.JPG|alt=|Honden File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii.jpg|alt=|Torii File:Museum of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Exterior view of Museum of Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Inari Shrine, Ebisu Shrine and Sarutahiko Shrine in Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gates of Inari, Ebisu, Sarutahiko Shrines in Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gate of Watatsumi Shrine File:View in Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|View within Watatsumi Shrine File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine parking lot.jpg|alt=|Watatsumi Shrine grounds File:View of Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden in Watatsumi Shrine File:Wadatsumi temple.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Watatusmi-Jinja.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine view of Haiden through Torii from street.jpg|alt=|Torii File:海神社鳥居 Gateway to Watatsumi-jinja shrine.jpg|alt=|Large Torii similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]] </gallery>

'''Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe) (Q11559098)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Akashi District, Hyōgo|en|Akashi District, Hyōgo|ja|明石郡|zh|明石郡|lt=Akashi district|WD=Q11512561}} || {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|lt=Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|en|Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|ja|海神社 (神戸市)|commons|Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)|simple|Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|WD=Q11559098}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}} ({{ill|Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|WD=Q135009132|lt=Tsukinami}}{{efn|gets offerings for the yearly {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|WD=Q11501518}} and the monthly {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|simple|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|WD=Q11516161}}.}}) || {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} || 4 seats || — || {{coord|34.628724|135.054257|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183009 DB] |} == Accidentally Overwritten Content ==
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)]][[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[ja:海神社 (神戸市)]][[simple:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11559098]] Wikidata name: Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine to the Sea God Watatsumi}} {{Citation style|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Watatsumi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg | deity = {{ill|Q112369714|WD=Q112369714}}; {{ill|Q112369870|WD=Q112369870}}; {{ill|Q112369995|WD=Q112369995}} | website = https://kaijinjya.main.jp/ | prefecture= {{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.629083|N|135.054306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyōgo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
It is colloquially called {{Nihongo|Sea Shrine|海神社|Umi Jinja}} due to that being a more common reading of the characters.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It is also read as Kai Shrine or called Tarumi Shrine.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}
== History ==
According to the shrine's legend, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was nearly shipwrecked when returning to Japan from a [[Empress Jingū#Korean Invasion|military conquest in Korea]]. She survived thanks to praying to [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], and made the shrine to honor him.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> There are 22 Watatsumi shrines in the region that claim to have been founded by her in these circumstances.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} [[Ikasuri Shrine]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]] were both also made at the same time by the Empress.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> The son of Tomomi no Sukune who accompanied the Empress on her expedition became the first priest of the shrine.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |pages=335 |language=English}}</ref>
In 806 AD, the shrine was financially supported by taxes from ten households.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]], the highest Rank of significant shrines in the 927 AD [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Watatsumi+Shrine |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] it gained a lot of significance for the rulers of the [[:en:Akashi Domain|Akashi Domain]], who visited it every February.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
Originally its main [[Torii]] was located on the beach like [[Itsukushima Shrine]], but due to land reclamation after [[:en:WWII|WWII]] its torii is now a fair bit inland.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> ==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Kai-jinja Shrine in Tarumi, Kobe File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|alt=|Donation box File:Kobe-wtatumijinjya76.JPG|alt=|Honden File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii.jpg|alt=|Torii File:Museum of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Exterior view of Museum of Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Inari Shrine, Ebisu Shrine and Sarutahiko Shrine in Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gates of Inari, Ebisu, Sarutahiko Shrines in Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gate of Watatsumi Shrine File:View in Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|View within Watatsumi Shrine File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine parking lot.jpg|alt=|Watatsumi Shrine grounds File:View of Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden in Watatsumi Shrine File:Wadatsumi temple.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Watatusmi-Jinja.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine view of Haiden through Torii from street.jpg|alt=|Torii File:海神社鳥居 Gateway to Watatsumi-jinja shrine.jpg|alt=|Large Torii similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]] </gallery>

'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[Empress Jingu]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyogo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />

In 806 AD, the shrine was financially supported by taxes from ten households.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]], the highest rank of significant shrines in the 927 AD [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Watatsumi+Shrine |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>

'''Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe) (Q11559098)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)]][[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[ja:海神社 (神戸市)]][[simple:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11559098]] Wikidata name: Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine to the Sea God Watatsumi}} {{Citation style|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Watatsumi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg | deity = {{ill|Q112369714|WD=Q112369714}}; {{ill|Q112369870|WD=Q112369870}}; {{ill|Q112369995|WD=Q112369995}} | website = https://kaijinjya.main.jp/ | prefecture= {{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.629083|N|135.054306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyōgo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
It is colloquially called {{Nihongo|Sea Shrine|海神社|Umi Jinja}} due to that being a more common reading of the characters.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It is also read as Kai Shrine or called Tarumi Shrine.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}
== History ==
According to the shrine's legend, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was nearly shipwrecked when returning to Japan from a [[Empress Jingū#Korean Invasion|military conquest in Korea]]. She survived thanks to praying to [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], and made the shrine to honor him.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> There are 22 Watatsumi shrines in the region that claim to have been founded by her in these circumstances.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} [[Ikasuri Shrine]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]] were both also made at the same time by the Empress.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> The son of Tomomi no Sukune who accompanied the Empress on her expedition became the first priest of the shrine.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |pages=335 |language=English}}</ref>
In 806 AD, the shrine was financially supported by taxes from ten households.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]], the highest Rank of significant shrines in the 927 AD [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Watatsumi+Shrine |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] it gained a lot of significance for the rulers of the [[:en:Akashi Domain|Akashi Domain]], who visited it every February.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
Originally its main [[Torii]] was located on the beach like [[Itsukushima Shrine]], but due to land reclamation after [[:en:WWII|WWII]] its torii is now a fair bit inland.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> ==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Kai-jinja Shrine in Tarumi, Kobe File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|alt=|Donation box File:Kobe-wtatumijinjya76.JPG|alt=|Honden File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii.jpg|alt=|Torii File:Museum of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Exterior view of Museum of Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Inari Shrine, Ebisu Shrine and Sarutahiko Shrine in Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gates of Inari, Ebisu, Sarutahiko Shrines in Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gate of Watatsumi Shrine File:View in Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|View within Watatsumi Shrine File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine parking lot.jpg|alt=|Watatsumi Shrine grounds File:View of Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden in Watatsumi Shrine File:Wadatsumi temple.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Watatusmi-Jinja.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine view of Haiden through Torii from street.jpg|alt=|Torii File:海神社鳥居 Gateway to Watatsumi-jinja shrine.jpg|alt=|Large Torii similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]] </gallery>

== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)]][[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[ja:海神社 (神戸市)]][[simple:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11559098]] Wikidata name: Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine to the Sea God Watatsumi}} {{Citation style|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Watatsumi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg | deity = {{ill|Q112369714|WD=Q112369714}}; {{ill|Q112369870|WD=Q112369870}}; {{ill|Q112369995|WD=Q112369995}} | website = https://kaijinjya.main.jp/ | prefecture= {{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.629083|N|135.054306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyōgo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
It is colloquially called {{Nihongo|Sea Shrine|海神社|Umi Jinja}} due to that being a more common reading of the characters.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It is also read as Kai Shrine or called Tarumi Shrine.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}
== History ==
According to the shrine's legend, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was nearly shipwrecked when returning to Japan from a [[Empress Jingū#Korean Invasion|military conquest in Korea]]. She survived thanks to praying to [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], and made the shrine to honor him.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> There are 22 Watatsumi shrines in the region that claim to have been founded by her in these circumstances.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} [[Ikasuri Shrine]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]] were both also made at the same time by the Empress.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> The son of Tomomi no Sukune who accompanied the Empress on her expedition became the first priest of the shrine.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |pages=335 |language=English}}</ref>
In 806 AD, the shrine was financially supported by taxes from ten households.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]], the highest Rank of significant shrines in the 927 AD [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Watatsumi+Shrine |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] it gained a lot of significance for the rulers of the [[:en:Akashi Domain|Akashi Domain]], who visited it every February.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
Originally its main [[Torii]] was located on the beach like [[Itsukushima Shrine]], but due to land reclamation after [[:en:WWII|WWII]] its torii is now a fair bit inland.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> ==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Kai-jinja Shrine in Tarumi, Kobe File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|alt=|Donation box File:Kobe-wtatumijinjya76.JPG|alt=|Honden File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii.jpg|alt=|Torii File:Museum of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Exterior view of Museum of Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Inari Shrine, Ebisu Shrine and Sarutahiko Shrine in Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gates of Inari, Ebisu, Sarutahiko Shrines in Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gate of Watatsumi Shrine File:View in Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|View within Watatsumi Shrine File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine parking lot.jpg|alt=|Watatsumi Shrine grounds File:View of Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden in Watatsumi Shrine File:Wadatsumi temple.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Watatusmi-Jinja.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine view of Haiden through Torii from street.jpg|alt=|Torii File:海神社鳥居 Gateway to Watatsumi-jinja shrine.jpg|alt=|Large Torii similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]] </gallery>

'''Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe) (Q11559098)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)]][[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[ja:海神社 (神戸市)]][[simple:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11559098]] Wikidata name: Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine to the Sea God Watatsumi}} {{Citation style|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Watatsumi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg | deity = {{ill|Q112369714|WD=Q112369714}}; {{ill|Q112369870|WD=Q112369870}}; {{ill|Q112369995|WD=Q112369995}} | website = https://kaijinjya.main.jp/ | prefecture= {{ill|Kobe|WD=Q48320}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.629083|N|135.054306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tarumi-ku, Kobe|Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyōgo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
It is colloquially called {{Nihongo|Sea Shrine|海神社|Umi Jinja}} due to that being a more common reading of the characters.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It is also read as Kai Shrine or called Tarumi Shrine.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}
== History ==
According to the shrine's legend, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] was nearly shipwrecked when returning to Japan from a [[Empress Jingū#Korean Invasion|military conquest in Korea]]. She survived thanks to praying to [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], and made the shrine to honor him.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> There are 22 Watatsumi shrines in the region that claim to have been founded by her in these circumstances.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} [[Ikasuri Shrine]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]] were both also made at the same time by the Empress.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> The son of Tomomi no Sukune who accompanied the Empress on her expedition became the first priest of the shrine.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |pages=335 |language=English}}</ref>
In 806 AD, the shrine was financially supported by taxes from ten households.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]], the highest Rank of significant shrines in the 927 AD [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Watatsumi+Shrine |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] it gained a lot of significance for the rulers of the [[:en:Akashi Domain|Akashi Domain]], who visited it every February.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
Originally its main [[Torii]] was located on the beach like [[Itsukushima Shrine]], but due to land reclamation after [[:en:WWII|WWII]] its torii is now a fair bit inland.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> ==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Kai-jinja Shrine in Tarumi, Kobe File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|alt=|Donation box File:Kobe-wtatumijinjya76.JPG|alt=|Honden File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii.jpg|alt=|Torii File:Museum of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Exterior view of Museum of Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Inari Shrine, Ebisu Shrine and Sarutahiko Shrine in Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gates of Inari, Ebisu, Sarutahiko Shrines in Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gate of Watatsumi Shrine File:View in Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|View within Watatsumi Shrine File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine parking lot.jpg|alt=|Watatsumi Shrine grounds File:View of Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden in Watatsumi Shrine File:Wadatsumi temple.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Watatusmi-Jinja.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine view of Haiden through Torii from street.jpg|alt=|Torii File:海神社鳥居 Gateway to Watatsumi-jinja shrine.jpg|alt=|Large Torii similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]] </gallery>

{{神社 |名称 = 海神社 |画像 = [[File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[垂水区]]宮本町5-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 上津綿津見神
中津綿津見神
底津綿津見神 |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[官幣中社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[神功皇后]]朝 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = 日向大明神 |札所等 = [[神仏霊場巡拝の道]]第73番（兵庫第8番）
播磨三大社 |例祭 = [[10月11日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Kobe|label=海神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''海神社'''（わたつみじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[垂水区]]宮本町にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]] ([[名神大社]])で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[官幣中社]]。現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。[[伊和神社]]・[[粒坐天照神社]]とともに[[播磨]]三大社とされる。 == 概要 == 宗教法人法による届け出では「わたつみじんじゃ」とされているが、「かいじんじゃ」ともいう読みも地元住民や氏子を含め広く知られている{{Cite web |title=神戸の「海神社」なんて読む？ 「かい」「あま」「わたつみ」… |url=https://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/odekake-plus/news/detail.shtml?url=news/odekake-plus/news/pickup/201712/10819940 |website=神戸新聞NEXT |date=2017-12-14 |access-date=2025-07-03 |language=Japanese}}{{国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|766190/1/21|山陽鉄道旅客案内 : 一名・山陽道名所図絵|format=EXTERNAL}}。古くは、あまじんじゃ・たるみじんじゃ、日向大明神、衣財田大明神。「ワタツミ」の読みは[[本居宣長]]の説に基づき[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に採用したものである。『播磨国官幣中社海神社史』では「古例の通りアマもしくはタルミと読むべきである」としている。「タルミ」の読みは、祭神の本来の名称が垂水神であったことによるものである。「アマ」は、当社が海直（あまのあたい）の[[氏神]]であったことによる。 == 祭神 == [[ワタツミ|海神]]三座として以下の3柱を主祭神とし、[[天照大神|大日孁貴尊]]を配祀する。 *上津綿津見神（うわつわたつみのかみ）：海上＝航海の神 *中津綿津見神（なかつわたつみのかみ）：海中＝魚（漁業）の神 *底津綿津見神（そこつわたつみのかみ）：海底＝海藻、塩の神 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、[[神功皇后]]が[[三韓征伐]]からの帰途、当地の海上で暴風雨が起こって船が進めなくなったため、皇后が綿津見三神を祀ると暴風雨が治まり、その縁でこの地に綿津見三神を祀る社殿を建てたのが始まりという。 『[[日本書紀]]』に記される[[廣田神社|広田神社]]・[[生田神社]]・[[長田神社]]・[[住吉大社]]創建の記述とほぼ同様であるが、『日本書紀』の当該箇所に海神社に関する記述はない。 文献に現れる最も古い記述は、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）の『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』にある播磨明石垂水神に[[神封戸]]10戸を寄進するという記述。 当所は海上交通の要地であることから、古くから海上鎮護の神として崇敬を受けた。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では「播磨国[[明石郡]] 海神社三座」と記載され、名神大社に列している。 中世以降、戦乱等のために社勢が衰えるが、[[天正]]11年（[[1587年]]）に[[豊臣秀吉]]が祈祷料として垂水郷山内の山林を寄進、江戸時代にも歴代[[明石藩]]主が篤く崇敬し、毎年2月に参拝するのを通例としていた。 江戸時代の初頭より「日向大明神」{{Cite book|和書|doi=10.11501/2563455|title=播州名所巡覧図会 二|date=1804|chapterurl={{NDLDC|2563455/24}}|chapter=垂水神社 日向明神}}と呼ばれていたが、明治4年（1871年）に[[国幣中社]]に列格した際に「海神社」に復称した。明治30年（[[1897年]]）に官幣中社に昇格した。 高度成長期前までは大鳥居周辺は砂浜であり、文字通り「海の神社」であったが、1960年以降埋め立てが行われて、大鳥居の前面は道路や漁港となった{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/kobe/202201/0014966735.shtml |title=垂水には砂浜、舞子は松が少しだけあった　昔と今、なぜ変わったの？ |publisher=神戸新聞NEXT |date=2022-01-07 |accessdate=2022-01-07}}。 == 祭祀 == === えびす祭 === [[西宮神社]]と同じく[[えびす]]は海の神でもあるため毎年[[1月10日]]の前後の3日間行われる。 == その他 == [[平成]]18年（[[2006年]]）まで、毎年[[7月]]に行われる夏祭りで奉納[[花火]]大会が開催されていたが、翌年以降夏祭りはこれまで通り開催されるものの奉納花火大会は開催されなくなった。{{要出典範囲|date=2012年1月|平成13年（2001年）に起きた[[明石花火大会歩道橋事故]]が少なからず影響している。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、65頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、359頁 == 関連項目 == *[[垂水区]] *[[垂水駅]] *[[マリンピア神戸]]・[[三井アウトレットパーク マリンピア神戸]] *[[プリコ垂水]] *[[下畑海神社]]（元宮） == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)}} * [http://kaijinjya.main.jp/index.php?FrontPage 海神社]（神社公式） * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301189.html 海神社]（兵庫県神社庁） * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/440501.html 海神社三座]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」） ファイル:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii (1).jpg|大鳥居 ファイル:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|海神社　寳ノ海神 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:神仏霊場巡拝の道]]

{{神社 |名称 = 海神社 |画像 = [[File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[垂水区]]宮本町5-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 上津綿津見神
中津綿津見神
底津綿津見神 |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[官幣中社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[神功皇后]]朝 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = 日向大明神 |札所等 = [[神仏霊場巡拝の道]]第73番（兵庫第8番）
播磨三大社 |例祭 = [[10月11日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Kobe|label=海神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''海神社'''（わたつみじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[垂水区]]宮本町にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]] ([[名神大社]])で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[官幣中社]]。現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。[[伊和神社]]・[[粒坐天照神社]]とともに[[播磨]]三大社とされる。 == 概要 == 宗教法人法による届け出では「わたつみじんじゃ」とされているが、「かいじんじゃ」ともいう読みも地元住民や氏子を含め広く知られている{{Cite web |title=神戸の「海神社」なんて読む？ 「かい」「あま」「わたつみ」… |url=https://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/odekake-plus/news/detail.shtml?url=news/odekake-plus/news/pickup/201712/10819940 |website=神戸新聞NEXT |date=2017-12-14 |access-date=2025-07-03 |language=Japanese}}{{国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|766190/1/21|山陽鉄道旅客案内 : 一名・山陽道名所図絵|format=EXTERNAL}}。古くは、あまじんじゃ・たるみじんじゃ、日向大明神、衣財田大明神。「ワタツミ」の読みは[[本居宣長]]の説に基づき[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に採用したものである。『播磨国官幣中社海神社史』では「古例の通りアマもしくはタルミと読むべきである」としている。「タルミ」の読みは、祭神の本来の名称が垂水神であったことによるものである。「アマ」は、当社が海直（あまのあたい）の[[氏神]]であったことによる。 == 祭神 == [[ワタツミ|海神]]三座として以下の3柱を主祭神とし、[[天照大神|大日孁貴尊]]を配祀する。 *上津綿津見神（うわつわたつみのかみ）：海上＝航海の神 *中津綿津見神（なかつわたつみのかみ）：海中＝魚（漁業）の神 *底津綿津見神（そこつわたつみのかみ）：海底＝海藻、塩の神 == 歴史 == 社伝によると、[[神功皇后]]が[[三韓征伐]]からの帰途、当地の海上で暴風雨が起こって船が進めなくなったため、皇后が綿津見三神を祀ると暴風雨が治まり、その縁でこの地に綿津見三神を祀る社殿を建てたのが始まりという。 『[[日本書紀]]』に記される[[廣田神社|広田神社]]・[[生田神社]]・[[長田神社]]・[[住吉大社]]創建の記述とほぼ同様であるが、『日本書紀』の当該箇所に海神社に関する記述はない。 文献に現れる最も古い記述は、[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）の『[[新抄格勅符抄]]』にある播磨明石垂水神に[[神封戸]]10戸を寄進するという記述。 当所は海上交通の要地であることから、古くから海上鎮護の神として崇敬を受けた。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』では「播磨国[[明石郡]] 海神社三座」と記載され、名神大社に列している。 中世以降、戦乱等のために社勢が衰えるが、[[天正]]11年（[[1587年]]）に[[豊臣秀吉]]が祈祷料として垂水郷山内の山林を寄進、江戸時代にも歴代[[明石藩]]主が篤く崇敬し、毎年2月に参拝するのを通例としていた。 江戸時代の初頭より「日向大明神」{{Cite book|和書|doi=10.11501/2563455|title=播州名所巡覧図会 二|date=1804|chapterurl={{NDLDC|2563455/24}}|chapter=垂水神社 日向明神}}と呼ばれていたが、明治4年（1871年）に[[国幣中社]]に列格した際に「海神社」に復称した。明治30年（[[1897年]]）に官幣中社に昇格した。 高度成長期前までは大鳥居周辺は砂浜であり、文字通り「海の神社」であったが、1960年以降埋め立てが行われて、大鳥居の前面は道路や漁港となった{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kobe-np.co.jp/news/kobe/202201/0014966735.shtml |title=垂水には砂浜、舞子は松が少しだけあった　昔と今、なぜ変わったの？ |publisher=神戸新聞NEXT |date=2022-01-07 |accessdate=2022-01-07}}。 == 祭祀 == === えびす祭 === [[西宮神社]]と同じく[[えびす]]は海の神でもあるため毎年[[1月10日]]の前後の3日間行われる。 == その他 == [[平成]]18年（[[2006年]]）まで、毎年[[7月]]に行われる夏祭りで奉納[[花火]]大会が開催されていたが、翌年以降夏祭りはこれまで通り開催されるものの奉納花火大会は開催されなくなった。{{要出典範囲|date=2012年1月|平成13年（2001年）に起きた[[明石花火大会歩道橋事故]]が少なからず影響している。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連図書 == * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』[[神社新報]]社、1968年、65頁 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、359頁 == 関連項目 == *[[垂水区]] *[[垂水駅]] *[[マリンピア神戸]]・[[三井アウトレットパーク マリンピア神戸]] *[[プリコ垂水]] *[[下畑海神社]]（元宮） == 外部リンク == [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/440501.html 海神社三座]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」） ファイル:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii (1).jpg|大鳥居 ファイル:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|海神社　寳ノ海神 {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)]][[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[ja:海神社 (神戸市)]][[simple:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Watatsumi-jinja (Kobe)]][[en:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]][[ja:海神社 (神戸市)]][[simple:Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine to the Sea God Watatsumi}} {{Citation style|date=November 2023}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Watatsumi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Torii of Inari Shrine, Ebisu Shrine and Sarutahiko Shrine in Watatsumi Shrine.jpg | deity = [[Watatsumi]] | website = https://kaijinjya.main.jp/ }}
'''Watatsumi Shrine''' (海神社, Watatsumi Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tarumi-ku, Kobe]].<ref name="imperial127">Ponsonby-Fane. ''Imperial,'' p. 127.</ref><ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} It is said to have been founded by the legendary [[Empress Jingu]] (169–269 AD).<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b">{{Cite web |title=Watatsumi Shrine {{!}} 海神社 {{!}}Hyogo-ken, Kobe-shi |url=https://www.rodsshinto.com/28-108-01 |access-date=2023-04-06 |website=shintoshrines |language=en}}</ref><ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a">{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}} It is one of the three major shrines of [[Harima Province]].<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It has a festival on October 11.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
It is colloquially called {{Nihongo|Sea Shrine|海神社|Umi Jinja}} due to that being a more common reading of the characters.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It is also read as Kai Shrine or called Tarumi Shrine.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}
== History ==
According to the shrine's legend, [[Empress Jingū]] was nearly shipwrecked when returning to Japan from a [[Empress Jingū#Korean Invasion|military conquest in Korea]]. She survived thanks to praying to [[Watatsumi]], and made the shrine to honor him.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> There are 22 Watatsumi shrines in the region that claim to have been founded by her in these circumstances.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} [[Ikasuri Shrine]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]] were both also made at the same time by the Empress.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> The son of Tomomi no Sukune who accompanied the Empress on her expedition became the first priest of the shrine.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |pages=335 |language=English}}</ref>
In 806 AD, the shrine was financially supported by taxes from ten households.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]], the highest rank of significant shrines in the 927 AD [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Watatsumi+Shrine |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
In the [[Edo Period]] it gained a lot of significance for the rulers of the [[Akashi Domain]], who visited it every February.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
Originally its main [[Torii]] was located on the beach like [[Itsukushima Shrine]], but due to land reclamation after [[WWII]] its torii is now a fair bit inland.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> ==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Kai-jinja Shrine in Tarumi, Kobe File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|alt=|Donation box File:Kobe-wtatumijinjya76.JPG|alt=|Honden File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii.jpg|alt=|Torii File:Museum of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Exterior view of Museum of Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Inari Shrine, Ebisu Shrine and Sarutahiko Shrine in Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gates of Inari, Ebisu, Sarutahiko Shrines in Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gate of Watatsumi Shrine File:View in Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|View within Watatsumi Shrine File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine parking lot.jpg|alt=|Watatsumi Shrine grounds File:View of Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden in Watatsumi Shrine File:Wadatsumi temple.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Watatusmi-Jinja.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine view of Haiden through Torii from street.jpg|alt=|Torii File:海神社鳥居 Gateway to Watatsumi-jinja shrine.jpg|alt=|Large Torii similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]] </gallery>
== See also ==
* [[Kaijin Shrine]] which is sometimes also known as "Watatsumi Jinja" * [[Shikaumi Shrine]] another major shrine to Watatsumi * [[Watazumi Shrine]]

[https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] * _______________. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/3994492 OCLC 399449] * _______________. (1963). [https://books.google.com/books?id=Uru8GMeblXgC&q=sapporo+shrine+yoshihisa-shinno ''The Viciissitudes of Shinto.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|186605327}} {{refend}}

She survived thanks to praying to [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]], and made the shrine to honor him.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}}<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> There are 22 Watatsumi shrines in the region that claim to have been founded by her in these circumstances.<ref name="wwwforgottenbookscom-2023a" />{{rp|7}} [[Ikasuri Shrine]] and [[Ikuta Shrine]] were both also made at the same time by the Empress.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> The son of Tomomi no Sukune who accompanied the Empress on her expedition became the first priest of the shrine.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |pages=335 |language=English}}</ref>
In 806 AD, the shrine was financially supported by taxes from ten households.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]], the highest Rank of significant shrines in the 927 AD [[Engishiki]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Watatsumi+Shrine |title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X |date=1972 |publisher=Sophia University |language=en}}</ref>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] it gained a lot of significance for the rulers of the [[:en:Akashi Domain|Akashi Domain]], who visited it every February.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" />
Originally its main [[Torii]] was located on the beach like [[Itsukushima Shrine]], but due to land reclamation after [[:en:WWII|WWII]] its torii is now a fair bit inland.<ref name="shintoshrines-2023b" /> ==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine File:Watatsumi-jinja (Tarumi, Kobe), haiden.jpg|alt=|Haiden of Kai-jinja Shrine in Tarumi, Kobe File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja altar.jpg|alt=|Donation box File:Kobe-wtatumijinjya76.JPG|alt=|Honden File:Kobe Watatsumi-jinja torii.jpg|alt=|Torii File:Museum of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Exterior view of Museum of Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Inari Shrine, Ebisu Shrine and Sarutahiko Shrine in Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gates of Inari, Ebisu, Sarutahiko Shrines in Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|Torii gate at Watatsumi Shrine File:Torii of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Torii gate of Watatsumi Shrine File:View in Watatsumi Shrine 2.jpg|alt=|View within Watatsumi Shrine File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine parking lot.jpg|alt=|Watatsumi Shrine grounds File:View of Haiden of Watatsumi Shrine.jpg|alt=|Haiden in Watatsumi Shrine File:Wadatsumi temple.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Watatusmi-Jinja.JPG|alt=|Torii File:Kobe Watatsumi Shrine view of Haiden through Torii from street.jpg|alt=|Torii File:海神社鳥居 Gateway to Watatsumi-jinja shrine.jpg|alt=|Large Torii similar to [[Itsukushima Shrine]] </gallery>
